chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Component
triplets.object: atomic raw materials or purchased parts

triplets.subject: Supplier
triplets.object: raw materials, components, or finished goods

triplets.subject: Supplier
triplets.object: negotiated pricing and lead times per component

triplets.subject: Warehouses
triplets.object: nodes in the supply chain network

triplets.subject: component
triplets.object: atomic raw materials and purchased parts

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is an atomic raw material or purchased part used in manufacturing that cannot be further broken down into simpler items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Contact Email Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A contact email address is an electronic mailing address used to communicate with an individual or organization.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: contact_email | VARCHAR(100) | | Contact email address

entities_post_resolution.name: Inspection Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The inspection date is the specific date on which an inspection is conducted or recorded in quality control processes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Inspection date | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory
entities_post_resolution.definition: Inventory represents the stock levels of components and products stored in warehouses, including details like quantity on hand, reserved quantities, and reorder thresholds.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date. | Products are stored in Inventory records

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A parent product is a product that serves as a component or assembly in a hierarchical structure, referencing other products as its components.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product for hierarchical assemblies | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique product identifier | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product represents any manufactured item, including finished goods, assemblies, and components, with attributes such as name, type, cost, and lead time.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Batches produce a specific Product | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child) | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. | Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | product_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Product name | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Completed
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity completed is a decimal value indicating the amount of work or items that have been finished or fulfilled in a process.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_completed | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0, <= ordered | Quantity completed |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity To Produce
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity to produce is the planned amount of product scheduled for manufacturing, whereas quantity produced is the actual amount of product manufactured.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_ordered | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity to produce | | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment is the physical transfer of materials between locations such as warehouses, suppliers, and customers within a supply chain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Shipments connect Warehouses, Suppliers, and Customers. | Shipments update Inventory levels.

entities_post_resolution.name: Technical Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A technical specification is a detailed document that defines the technical requirements, standards, and criteria for materials, products, or processes to ensure quality and compliance.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Technical specification reference | QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing a supplier, including its unique identifier and name, used in business data to identify and reference suppliers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | Suppliers are evaluated based on performance ratings, preferred status for strategic partnerships, and maintain contact information for procurement operations. | Suppliers are linked to Inbound Shipments | Suppliers have negotiated pricing and lead times per component | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Of Measure
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unit of measure is a standard quantity used to quantify and specify the amount of a product or material, such as EA, KG, M, or L.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.)

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Warehouse is a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored, managed, and used in supply chain operations.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A work order is a formal document or record that authorizes and details a specific production job or manufacturing task to produce a designated quantity of a product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Work Orders track planned versus completed quantities, status through the production lifecycle, priority levels, and planned dates. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Job
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production job is a specific manufacturing task or process assigned to produce a certain quantity of a product within a manufacturing system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

entities_post_resolution.name: Standard Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: A standard cost is a predetermined estimated cost used for budgeting, cost control, and financial analysis in procurement and manufacturing processes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufactured Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufactured item is any product created through a manufacturing process, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, and components.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components.

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: A category of basic raw materials and purchased components used in manufacturing processes that cannot be further broken down into simpler items.
synonyms: atomic raw materials or purchased parts
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Supplier Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: component_id: a unique identifier for a component; component_product_id: an identifier linking a component to its product; component_supplier_id: a unique identifier for the relationship between a component and its supplier.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it)

entities_post_resolution.name: Expiry Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: An expiry date is the date after which a product, especially perishable goods, is no longer considered safe or suitable for use or sale.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches may have expiry dates for perishable goods

entities_post_resolution.name: Hierarchical Product Relationships
entities_post_resolution.definition: Hierarchical product relationships are the structured associations that define parent-child or decomposition relationships among products or work orders in a manufacturing or product management context.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: End Dates
entities_post_resolution.definition: These entities represent the scheduled start and end dates for a project or activity within a planning or scheduling context.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_end_date | DATE | | Planned end date | | | planned_start_date | DATE | | Planned start date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Priority
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute indicating the level of urgency or importance, typically with values like LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, and URGENT.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | priority | priority_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'MEDIUM' | LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT |

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: qc_type refers to the category or classification of quality control processes or stages within a business or manufacturing workflow.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute that indicates the current state or phase of an entity or process, such as pending, in progress, completed, or closed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | status | status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLOSED |

entities_post_resolution.name: Defect Count
entities_post_resolution.definition: The defect_count represents the number of defects identified during quality inspections, used to assess product quality.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | defect_count | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Number of defects found | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Performance Rating
entities_post_resolution.definition: A performance rating is a quantitative score used to evaluate the effectiveness or quality of a supplier, employee, or process based on specific criteria.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: rating | DECIMAL(3,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 3.00, CHECK 1.0-5.0 | Performance rating | Suppliers are evaluated based on performance ratings, preferred status for strategic partnerships, and maintain contact information for procurement operations.

entities_post_resolution.name: Cycle Time
entities_post_resolution.definition: Cycle time is the total time required to complete a process or operation from start to finish, including setup and processing durations.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.

entities_post_resolution.name: Default 0.00
entities_post_resolution.definition: A default value assigned to a database field or variable, which varies depending on the data type and context.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time

entities_post_resolution.name: Bom
entities_post_resolution.definition: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child)
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child)

entities_post_resolution.name: Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: Batches produce a specific Product | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches produce a specific Product | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Bill Of Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Depth In Product Hierarchy
entities_post_resolution.definition: bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy

entities_post_resolution.definition: Suppliers are linked to Inbound Shipments
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Suppliers are linked to Inbound Shipments

entities_post_resolution.definition: | inspector_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Inspector reference |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | inspector_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Inspector reference |

entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipments update Inventory levels.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Shipments update Inventory levels.

entities_post_resolution.name: Negotiated Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition: unit_price | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Negotiated unit price
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: unit_price | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Negotiated unit price

entities_post_resolution.definition: | parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition |

entities_post_resolution.definition: is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier flag
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier flag

entities_post_resolution.definition: is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier for this component
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier for this component

entities_post_resolution.definition: lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time

entities_post_resolution.definition: | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |

entities_post_resolution.name: Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.

entities_post_resolution.name: Physical Storage Location
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored.

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Identifier
entities_post_resolution.definition: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Acceptable Value Range

entities_post_resolution.name: Either Components Or Products
entities_post_resolution.definition: Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date.

entities_post_resolution.name: Inspection Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about products, including their identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships, costs, lead times, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about various components used in manufacturing or assembly processes, including their identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, costs, and related specifications... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: This table stores the components and structure of a product's bill of materials, detailing which components are used in each product and their quantities.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about suppliers providing products or services to the company, including contact details and preference status.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about warehouse locations, including their identifiers, names, addresses, capacities, and managers responsible for each warehouse.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: This table tracks the current inventory levels, stock status, and restocking information for products stored across various warehouses.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or maintenance work orders, including their status, priorities, associated products, quantities, and scheduling details.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: This table records details about shipments, including their types, associated warehouses, suppliers, customers, and key dates related to shipment scheduling and delivery status.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: This table stores records of quality control inspections, including details about inspection batches, specifications, inspectors, results, and any defects identified.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: This table stores specifications details, including versioning, effective dates, and critical parameters for various products or components.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: This table records information about manufacturing batches, including production details, quantities, storage locations, and quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or process routes, including the sequence of operations, associated products, and timing details.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about the suppliers for each component, including whether they are preferred suppliers, the lead time for delivery, and the unit price of components.
